sources.notes: For each range query, output all values whose frequency is more than one third of the range length.
Official editorial presents both randomized candidate sampling and a deterministic Misra-Gries/segment-tree solution.

annotation.summary: Answer range queries asking for every value appearing more than |range|/3 times.
annotation.constraints: n,q are large, and each query can have at most two valid answers, so only a constant number of candidates should be verified.
annotation.core_idea: Use the Misra-Gries majority-vote algorithm with capacity 2: merging two summaries preserves all elements that could exceed one third frequency. Build a segment tree whose node stores the two Misra-Gries candidates for that segment, merge summaries along the query range, then verify each candidate's true frequency by binary searching its occurrence-position list.
annotation.complexity: O(log n) candidate retrieval plus O(log n) verification per candidate.

solution_variants.name: misra-gries-segment-tree
solution_variants.summary: Store two Misra-Gries candidates per segment-tree node, merge them for a query, and verify candidates with occurrence lists.

solution_variants.name: random-sampling
solution_variants.summary: Sample several random positions from the range and verify their frequencies; probabilistic but simpler.

tags.evidence: The deterministic solution stores and merges Misra-Gries summaries with two candidates to preserve all >len/3 elements.
tags.description: Generalized majority-vote algorithm that keeps k-1 counters to find all elements with frequency greater than n/k; also useful as a mergeable range summary.
tags.created_reason: Range majority and heavy-hitter problems are a recurring category not captured by generic data-structure tags.

tags.evidence: Segment tree merging is used to obtain the Misra-Gries summary for any query range.
Candidate frequencies are verified by lower_bound/upper_bound on occurrence-position lists.
The randomized sampling solution is an alternative, but the deterministic reviewed solution does not depend on probability.
